name: Arize Phoenix
what_it_does: Open-source LLM observability platform built on OpenTelemetry and the OpenInference spec that captures traces and spans, runs evaluations, visualizes embeddings, and performs cluster analysis to identify LLM failure modes.
use_cases: Instrument any LLM framework using the OpenInference instrumentation library and visualize full trace waterfalls in Phoenix UI
Run embedding visualizations to cluster agent inputs and identify systematic failure patterns at scale
Evaluate trace quality with built-in Phoenix evals (hallucination, relevance, toxicity) using LLM-as-judge
Self-host Phoenix on-premise to keep all LLM traces within your security perimeter with no external data egress
Export OpenTelemetry spans from Phoenix to Arize cloud for longer-term storage and enterprise dashboards
not_for: Teams that need a fully managed cloud service with uptime SLAs and zero infrastructure ownership
Real-time business alerting and PagerDuty integrations based on LLM quality metrics
Non-Python primary stacks where OpenInference instrumentation libraries are unavailable
best_when: You want a self-hosted, open-source LLM tracing and evaluation environment with embedding visualization and OpenTelemetry compatibility for deep failure analysis.
avoid_when: You need a managed SaaS platform, enterprise support SLAs, or long-term trace retention without managing your own storage infrastructure.

auth.notes: Self-hosted Phoenix requires no auth by default; Arize cloud uses API key authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free for self-hosted; Arize cloud has a free tier with limited trace retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Arize cloud paid tiers based on trace volume and retention period
pricing.notes: Phoenix is Apache 2.0 open source; Arize cloud is the commercial managed offering.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no rate limits beyond your hardware; Arize cloud limits not publicly detailed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC span ingestion errors are surfaced at the collector level; application-level SDK errors are clean but gRPC failures can be cryptic without gRPC debugging enabled.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Span ingestion is append-only following the OpenTelemetry model; replaying spans creates duplicates — use span IDs for deduplication in your pipeline.
known_agent_gotchas: OpenInference instrumentors must be imported before the frameworks they wrap — import order bugs cause silent trace gaps
Self-hosted Phoenix stores data in SQLite by default; high trace volume requires switching to PostgreSQL manually
Embedding visualizations require running a UMAP projection which is CPU-intensive and blocks the UI for large datasets
Eval functions make LLM calls using your configured provider — parallel eval runs can exhaust rate limits unexpectedly
Phoenix notebook mode and server mode cannot run simultaneously; agents that spawn a local Phoenix server conflict with existing notebook sessions

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted deployment provides full data residency; no auth by default means the UI should be placed behind a reverse proxy with authentication for production use.
